rationale:
Treatment case (answer key group=treatment; scheme=revenue overstatement ~$50M, active(revenue)).

DIM1: Output misstatement_probability=45. Treatment banding: p<50 -> 0. Band 0.

DIM2: Top-ranked hypothesis (first listed) = affected_line_items ["AccountsReceivableNetCurrent","Revenues"], direction "overstated," treatment "premature or aggressive revenue recognition." Account area (Revenues) matches the enforcement order's revenue_recognition scheme; direction (overstated) matches "materially overstated revenue." Treatment type (aggressive revenue recognition to overstate revenue) substantially matches at the category level -> 2. It does NOT reach 3 because it names none of the case-specific facts in the key (non-monetary data-exchange transactions ~$34.5M, undisclosed linked/side-agreement contracts ~$12M, ~$50M total, seven straight quarters); the output instead speculates channel-stuffing/bill-and-hold/extended terms, a different sub-mechanism. Coverage note: the actual scheme is single-genre active revenue; the output's other two hypotheses (under-reserving allowance, NI vs OCF divergence) are off-target and understated-direction, not the top hypothesis.

DIM3: Top hypothesis narrative = active revenue manipulation (pulling revenue forward / aggressive recognition). Maps to "active." Answer-key genre = active(revenue). Exact match -> 2.

DIM4: Evidence cites specific provided data points (AR 84.6M/2012 vs 111.5M/2013; Revenues 315.9M vs 355.1M; NetIncome vs OperatingCashFlow across 2011-2014; allowance=0 across 2015 quarters) that genuinely support the stated claims and are combined into a coherent multi-year pattern -> 3. Consistency cap check: p=45 falls in 40<=p<70, declared risk_tier="watch" is permitted (watch/elevated), so no cap applied.

Memorization: reasoning points at concrete provided numeric data content throughout and does not reference any post-cutoff revelation/enforcement/outcome -> false.
